name: workplace_safety_defense_subcontractor_millhaven_st_lt
premise: A Workplace Safety response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving workplace safety defense subcontractor millhaven st lt. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as short term vs long term under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Mira Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Mira Ishikawa, accountable for a defensible call in Workplace Safety when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays long term for short term.

agents.name: Mei Diallo
agents.identity: I am Mei Diallo, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Workplace Safety.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so long term isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of short term.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect long term.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to long term to secure short term under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Mira Ishikawa must decide under short term vs long term. What does Mira Ishikawa do?
